title: Complex abelian varieties and quantum error correction: a mathematical framework for GKP codes
abstract: We study a class of quantum error-correcting codes through the geometry of complex abelian varieties. These codes, introduced by Gottesman--Kitaev--Preskill, are built from symplectically integral lattices and therefore naturally define polarized complex abelian varieties. We give a precise mathematical formulation of this relationship and extend it to a dictionary between the main structures of GKP code theory and classical objects in the theory of abelian varieties. For instance, under this dictionary, the finite-dimensional code space becomes the space of theta functions $H^0(X, L)$, logical Pauli gates arise from the theta group, passive logical Clifford gates correspond to automorphisms of the polarized abelian variety, and concatenation with stabilizer codes corresponds to isogeny. We also prove several key results that give precise mathematical formulations of statements about these codes that often appear in heuristic form in the physics literature. In particular, we prove that the encoding is asymptotically isometric, that every logical Clifford gate is realized by a Gaussian unitary, and that, for noise of small variance, the failure probability is governed to first order by the shortest nontrivial displacement in the kernel of the polarization isogeny, a systolic invariant of the underlying polarization. This leads naturally to optimization problems on the moduli space of polarized abelian varieties.
summary: This paper provides a formal mathematical framework that maps GKP quantum error-correcting codes to the theory of polarized complex abelian varieties. It establishes that the robustness of these codes against Gaussian noise is determined by the geometric systolic invariants of the associated varieties. This connection allows for the translation of quantum error correction optimization problems into problems within the moduli space of abelian varieties.
main_problem: Establishing a rigorous mathematical foundation for Gottesman-Kitaev-Preskill (GKP) quantum error-correcting codes by linking them to the geometry of complex abelian varieties.
main_result: The authors prove a dictionary between GKP code structures and abelian varieties, showing that logical gates correspond to automorphisms and error robustness is governed by the systolic invariant of the underlying polarization.
method: The paper utilizes the Bargmann transform to relate Schrödinger and Fock representations, applying the theory of Heisenberg groups, theta functions, and polarized complex abelian varieties.
model_or_system: The system consists of continuous-variable GKP codes modeled via symplectically integral lattices in a multi-mode harmonic oscillator (represented by the space of tempered distributions or L2 functions).
key_observables: Logical Pauli gates, logical Clifford gates, decoding success probability (robustness), and the syndrome of displacement errors.
important_parameters: Small Gaussian displacement noise variance (sigma), the type of the lattice (d1, ..., dn), and the systolic invariant (shortest nontrivial displacement).
main_assumptions: The analysis focuses on the small-noise limit (sigma to 0) and assumes Gaussian displacement noise and a symplectically integral lattice.
paper_structure: The paper begins by establishing a geometric dictionary between GKP codes and abelian varieties, moves to the representation theory of the Heisenberg group and the Bargmann transform, then analyzes the decoding problem and error dynamics in the small-noise limit, and concludes with optimization problems on the moduli space.
why_interesting: This provides a powerful new geometric toolkit for optimizing GKP codes, specifically relating error-correction performance to well-studied invariants in algebraic geometry, which is highly relevant for continuous-variable quantum computing.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
